title: Treehouse Of Horror V

jokes.text: Oh, my. It seems the show is so scary, Congress won't even let us show it.
jokes.setup: Standard Halloween special warning about scary content
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Congress censors fictional cartoon content
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on censorship that breaks the fourth wall effectively

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Some of the content you are about to see is so shocking and disturbing, it may cause nightmares, severe psychological damage, and in extreme cases, short-term memory loss.

dialogue.speaker: Announcer
dialogue.line: The following program contains scenes of violence, peril, and some instances of mild language.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: We urge parental discretion. But to be safe, we've notified Congress and they've agreed to censor all fictional cartoon content.

jokes.text: Instead they've suggested the 1947 classic Glenn Ford movie... Two Hundred Miles to Oregon.
jokes.setup: Congress suggesting alternative programming
jokes.punchline: A completely fictional, boring-sounding old movie
jokes.explanation: Solid absurdist humor with the made-up movie title that sounds authentically boring

dialogue.speaker: Congressman
dialogue.line: We could show them an old movie instead.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh, that's a wonderful idea! What movie?

dialogue.speaker: Congressman
dialogue.line: How about 'The Lusty Appetites of Veronica Handbasket'?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: No, no, something more wholesome.

dialogue.speaker: Congressman
dialogue.line: We could show 'Paint Drying: A Study in Beige.'

jokes.text: What's that, boy? We're in control? Hey, look! I can see my voice!
jokes.setup: Parody of Outer Limits opening with serious tone
jokes.punchline: Homer completely missing the point and getting excited about seeing sound waves
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - takes serious sci-fi concept and gets childishly excited about the wrong thing

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: There is nothing wrong with your television set. Do not attempt to adjust the picture. We are now controlling the transmission. We will control all that you see and hear.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wow! We're gonna see sound waves? Cool!

jokes.text: Dad, you're ruining the mood! - Sorry.
jokes.setup: Serious horror introduction being undermined
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately apologizing when called out
jokes.explanation: Classic family dynamic with Homer being appropriately chastened

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: In the night, evil stirs. A darkness that feeds on the innocent. A malevolent force that—

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Sorry! Sorry, I'll be quiet.

jokes.text: Well, it's been two long trips, but we're finally almost there again.
jokes.setup: Homer just said they were almost there after a long trip
jokes.punchline: Immediately having to repeat the same line after forgetting to lock the door
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure showing Homer's resignation to his own incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We're almost there!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We're almost there!

jokes.text: Oh, no! We left Grampa back at the gas station!
jokes.setup: Family realizing they forgot things
jokes.punchline: Forgetting a family member is treated as casually as forgetting to lock doors
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about family neglect of the elderly, escalating from doors to people

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Did we forget anything?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: I don't think so.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Wait, where's Lisa?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh well, we'll catch her next time.

jokes.text: What about Grampa?
jokes.setup: Marge worried about leaving Grampa behind
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete indifference to the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer callousness in just three words

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, we can't just leave your father behind!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why not? He left me behind at the mall once.

jokes.text: Look at them cavort and caper.
jokes.setup: Burns commenting on new arrivals
jokes.punchline: Using fancy antiquated language to describe normal human behavior
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns vocabulary joke - treating people like exotic animals

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Ah yes, the new employees. I see they engage in 'small talk' — how quaint. And there, one of them is 'micturating' in the lavatory. Splendid use of our facilities. And observe — that one is 'masticating' a sandwich. The way they persist in these primitive rituals is simply fascinating.

jokes.text: ...and five John Denver Christmas specials.
jokes.setup: List of increasingly horrible things that happened in the house
jokes.punchline: John Denver Christmas specials as the ultimate horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke - treating cheesy TV specials as worse than satanic rituals

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: The worst thing that ever happened in this house was when we had to watch John Denver Christmas specials.

jokes.text: John Denver.
jokes.setup: Horror at John Denver Christmas specials
jokes.punchline: Homer's deadpan acknowledgment of the true horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing with just the name delivery

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, we've been through a lot this Christmas, but nothing — and I mean nothing — is as terrifying as a John Denver Christmas special.

jokes.text: That's odd. Usually the blood gets off at the second floor.
jokes.setup: Supernatural elevator behavior in haunted house
jokes.punchline: Treating sentient blood as a regular passenger with a routine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion treating horror as mundane routine - highly quotable and original

dialogue.speaker: Elevator Operator
dialogue.line: Going up?

dialogue.speaker: Burns
dialogue.line: Yes, to the executive suite.

dialogue.speaker: Elevator Operator
dialogue.line: That'll be the third floor. Watch your step.

dialogue.speaker: Burns
dialogue.line: What is that red substance?

dialogue.speaker: Elevator Operator
dialogue.line: Oh, that's just sentient blood. Regular passenger. Gets off at two.

jokes.text: Hey, I found a shortcut through your hedge maze.
jokes.setup: Traditional hedge maze as elaborate puzzle
jokes.punchline: Bart simply cutting through it instead of solving it
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart problem-solving - direct and destructive, perfect character moment

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Forget this maze puzzle stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: I'm just cutting through it.

jokes.text: What's haggis?
jokes.setup: Willie threatening to chop them into haggis
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent question about what sounds like a threat
jokes.explanation: Good setup for cultural ignorance joke, though fairly standard

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: What's a haggis?

jokes.text: You mean shining. - You want to get sued?
jokes.setup: Bart correcting the pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Willie breaking fourth wall about copyright concerns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about legal issues that works on multiple levels - highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: It's pronounced 'Jag-you-ar', not 'Jag-wire'.

dialogue.speaker: Willie
dialogue.line: Aye, well I dinnae care how ye pronounce it. I'm just worried about gettin' sued by Jaguar for mentionin' their product on this show without proper compensation!

jokes.text: But don't be reading my mind between 4 and 5. That's Willie's time!
jokes.setup: Willie explaining psychic communication
jokes.punchline: Implying he has private thoughts during specific hours
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific awkward humor suggesting Willie's personal habits

dialogue.speaker: Willie
dialogue.line: Aye, I can communicate with the spirits through me mind, but only between the hours of 3 and 5 PM on Tuesdays.

jokes.text: I owe you a Coke.
jokes.setup: Burns acknowledging his plan might cause mass murder
jokes.punchline: Treating potential family slaughter as a minor bet
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - treating human tragedy as trivial wager with casual indifference

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: Yes, well, if a little mass murder is the price of a 50-50 chance at profit, then so be it.

dialogue.speaker: Smithers
dialogue.line: Sir, I think you mean a 50-50 chance at a profit.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Burns
dialogue.line: That's what I said, you idiot.

jokes.text: Not a drop in the house. What do you know.
jokes.setup: Homer looking for beer after it was removed
jokes.punchline: Feigning surprise at the predictable outcome
jokes.explanation: Good Homer sarcasm showing he understood the setup all along

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Where's my beer? I demand to know what happened to it!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, I threw it out. You need to cut back.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: What?! No! This is the worst day of my life!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, you say that every day.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh... well, if I'm going to have a bad day, I might as well have a beer. Wait, there's no beer!

jokes.text: I'll kill you! I'll kill all of you! - Homer! - Sorry. Sorry. Don't worry.
jokes.setup: Homer handling withdrawal well
jokes.punchline: Immediate violent outburst followed by sheepish apology
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's instant mood swings and Marge's resigned patience

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why you little!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, honey. I didn't mean it.

jokes.text: Maybe I'll check out that ax collection.
jokes.setup: Homer looking for activities to occupy himself
jokes.punchline: Innocent interest in weapons while in murderous mood
jokes.explanation: Good ominous setup delivered with Homer's characteristic obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I need something to do. Maybe I'll take up a hobby.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Let's see... I could learn about weapons. Yeah, that's a good hobby.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Now where did I put that gun...

jokes.text: Mom, is Dad gonna kill us? - We're just gonna have to wait and see.
jokes.setup: Child's reasonable concern about father's murderous threats
jokes.punchline: Marge's matter-of-fact acceptance of potential family murder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with Marge's casual acceptance of domestic violence as inevitable

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Mom, Dad's been making murderous threats again.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh, that's just your father being your father.

jokes.text: They'd be much happier as ghosts.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why he should kill his family
jokes.punchline: Treating murder as a favor to the victims
jokes.explanation: Good dark logic twist suggesting murder as kindness

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Why should I kill my family?

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Because you'd be doing them a favor!

jokes.text: 'Feeling fine.'
jokes.setup: Marge checking Homer's writing as 'window into his madness'
jokes.punchline: Completely normal, reassuring message
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection that subverts expectation of crazy writing. Sets up the next revelation nicely.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, what is this? Some kind of window into your madness?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Just read it, Marge.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: "Dear Marge, I love you. Homer."

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: See? Completely normal. Reassuring.

jokes.text: This is less encouraging.
jokes.setup: Previous reassuring message from Homer
jokes.punchline: Understatement about clearly threatening text (likely 'All work and no play')
jokes.explanation: Classic understatement humor in face of obvious danger

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, that message is a little scary.

jokes.text: No TV and No Beer Make Homer something, something. - Go Crazy? - Don't mind if I do!
jokes.setup: Homer forgetting the rest of the famous phrase
jokes.punchline: Taking Marge's completion as a polite suggestion to accept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of famous horror quote with perfect Homer logic - became iconic meme

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, Marge, I'm like the great Vasco da Gama of explorers. I have crossed oceans, I have braved the unknown, I have—

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: —faced seemingly impossible odds?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, okay. If you say so.

jokes.text: Chili would be good tonight.
jokes.setup: Just locked away homicidal husband
jokes.punchline: Casual dinner planning immediately after traumatic event
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - domestic normalcy in face of absolute chaos

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, I suppose I should think about what to make for dinner tonight.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer does love my pork chops.

jokes.text: David Letterman! - Hi, David. I'm Grampa.
jokes.setup: Homer doing the famous 'Here's Johnny' line
jokes.punchline: Wrong TV host reference plus Grampa's confused greeting
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with wrong reference plus perfect Grampa confusion

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Here's Johnny!

dialogue.speaker: Grampa
dialogue.line: Who's Johnny? I'm looking for Homer.

jokes.text: I'm Mike Wallace. I'm Morley Safer. And I'm Ed Bradley. All this and Andy Rooney tonight on 60 Minutes!
jokes.setup: Horror movie parody moment
jokes.punchline: Homer doing 60 Minutes introduction instead
jokes.explanation: Surreal non-sequitur that completely derails horror atmosphere

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm not a smart man, but I know what love is.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.line: This week on 60 Minutes...

jokes.text: Well, thank God that's over. I was worried there for a second.
jokes.setup: No answer from police call
jokes.punchline: Marge's complete misreading of the situation
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Marge's misplaced relief

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Hello, police? My husband is missing!

dialogue.speaker: Police Dispatcher
dialogue.line: We'll send someone right away, ma'am.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh thank God. He's been gone for three hours. I'm so worried!

dialogue.speaker: Police Dispatcher
dialogue.line: Three hours? That's not unusual. He'll probably turn up.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: But what if something terrible happened? What if he's been in an accident?

dialogue.speaker: Police Dispatcher
dialogue.line: Ma'am, calm down. Does your husband have a job?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Yes, at the nuclear power plant.

dialogue.speaker: Police Dispatcher
dialogue.line: He's probably just at work.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Oh! Well, that explains it. He must have gotten a raise and decided to celebrate by not coming home.

jokes.text: Oh, my. I hope that rug was Scotchgarded.
jokes.setup: Willie just got axed in the back
jokes.punchline: Concern about carpet protection while dying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan priorities joke - caring about housekeeping while being murdered

dialogue.speaker: Willie
dialogue.line: Och, I've been axed in the back! But worry about the carpet, ye daft eejit!

jokes.text: Television! Teacher! Mother! Secret lover.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing TV and calming down
jokes.punchline: Escalating relationships with television ending in 'secret lover'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment showing his relationship with TV through escalating intimacy

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh, a new TV guide! Let me see what's on.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hmm, nothing good. I'll just watch static.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You know, TV, you've been there for me through thick and thin.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We've shared so many memories together.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I think... I think I love you, TV.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: In fact, you're not just my friend anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You're my best friend.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: No, you're more than that...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: You're my secret lover.

jokes.text: Come, family. Sit in the snow with Daddy... and let us all bask in television's warm, glowing, warming glow.
jokes.setup: Family reconciliation moment
jokes.punchline: Sitting in snow to watch TV plus redundant 'warm/warming' description
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with Homer's poetic but redundant TV worship

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Kids, your mother and I have decided to sit out here in the snow and watch TV together as a family.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: But Dad, it's freezing out here.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I know, son. That's why we're sitting so close together to keep warm and warming each other up.

jokes.text: Homer, change channel. - Can't. Frozen.
jokes.setup: Watching terrible awards show
jokes.punchline: Homer literally frozen but also metaphorically stuck watching bad TV
jokes.explanation: Good double meaning with physical and metaphorical freezing

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, why aren't you changing the channel?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I can't, Marge. I'm frozen.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Frozen? It's 72 degrees in here.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Not physically frozen. Metaphorically frozen. By the sheer mediocrity of this awards show. I've lost the will to move.

jokes.text: I'm the first non-Brazilian person to travel backwards through time. - Correction, Homer. You're the second. - That's right, Mr. Peabody. - Quiet, you.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming to be first time traveler
jokes.punchline: Reference to Mr. Peabody and Sherman cartoon plus dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference with dismissive follow-up

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'm a time traveler. First time, actually.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Peabody
dialogue.line: Well, you're not the first. I've been doing this with my son Sherman for years.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mr. Peabody and Sherman? Pfft, that's just a stupid cartoon.

jokes.text: If you ever travel back in time, don't step on anything. Because even the tiniest change... can alter the future in ways you can't imagine.
jokes.setup: Father's wedding day advice about time travel
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific advice that's actually relevant
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup for coming gag with absurd but prescient paternal wisdom

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Son, on your wedding day, remember: if you ever travel back in time, don't step on any bugs, don't change history, and whatever you do, don't mess with your parents' first kiss.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad, why would I travel back in time on my wedding day?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I don't know, but when you do, you'll thank me. Now get out there and try not to erase yourself from existence!

jokes.text: Stupid bug! You go squish now!
jokes.setup: Homer just received specific warning about not touching anything
jokes.punchline: Immediately crushing a mosquito with caveman-like language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer impulsiveness undermining wise advice immediately

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! Why must you tempt me?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Ugh, a mosquito! Must... destroy... *slaps wildly* Unghhh!

jokes.text: Hidilly-ho, slave-a-reenos.
jokes.setup: Returning to altered timeline
jokes.punchline: Flanders as dictator but still using his characteristic speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character consistency in alternate universe - evil Flanders still talks like himself

dialogue.speaker: Flanders
dialogue.line: Well howdy-doodly, citizens! Time to implement some mandatory fun-diddly-un activities!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh no, not Flanders as a dictator!

dialogue.speaker: Flanders
dialogue.line: That's right-a-rooney, Homer! All citizens must report for forced niceness at oh-eight-hundred hours-diddly-ours, or face the tickle-torture chamber!

jokes.text: I see by the big board we got a Negative Nelly in sector two.
jokes.setup: Dictator Flanders addressing dissent
jokes.punchline: Using cutesy terminology for authoritarian surveillance
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between Flanders' nice-guy language and dystopian control

dialogue.speaker: Flanders
dialogue.line: Now listen here, you diddly-doodly dissenters! I've installed cameras on every corner-diddly-orner of this town!

dialogue.speaker: Flanders
dialogue.line: I like to call it 'neighborly observation' and 'community peep-a-rooney!'

dialogue.speaker: Flanders
dialogue.line: Your privacy is being violated in the most Christian way possible!

jokes.text: It's not so bad, Homer. They go in through your nose... and they let you keep the piece of brain they cut out.
jokes.setup: Lobotomy being performed
jokes.punchline: Treating brain surgery as a thoughtful keepsake service
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical procedure with customer service angle

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: A lobotomy? That'll be $50.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: That's it? I thought it'd be more.

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Well, I'll throw in a nice frame. You know, so you can hang your brain on the wall. A real keepsake.

jokes.text: These wieners will give me the quick energy I need to escape!
jokes.setup: Homer being chased and needing escape plan
jokes.punchline: Stopping to eat hot dogs as athletic fuel
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food logic in the middle of life-or-death situation

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I gotta lose these guys! I need to find an escape route!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait... hot dogs. Hot dogs are what I need. Gotta keep my energy up if I'm gonna outrun 'em.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... athletic fuel.

jokes.text: I wish, I wish I hadn't killed that fish
jokes.setup: Homer trying not to change anything
jokes.punchline: Accidentally killing a fish while being careful
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the butterfly effect concept

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Okay, I'll just be very careful and not change anything.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I'll just stand here real still...

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Oh no! I killed the fish!

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But I wasn't even doing anything!

jokes.text: Hey, there's a bug that looks like Dad. - Let's kill it. - Okay.
jokes.setup: Homer transformed into prehistoric creature
jokes.punchline: His own children casually deciding to kill him
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with family dynamics in altered reality

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Dad's a monster. We gotta kill him.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Agreed.

jokes.text: Fabulous house, well-behaved kids, sisters-in-law dead, luxury sedan... I hit the jackpot!
jokes.setup: Homer listing benefits of this timeline
jokes.punchline: Including dead in-laws as a positive feature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities showing what he really values in life

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, there's free donuts at work, Marge finally loves me, and best of all... my in-laws are dead!

jokes.text: Doughnut? What's a doughnut?
jokes.setup: Homer asking for normal breakfast item
jokes.punchline: Doughnuts don't exist in this timeline
jokes.explanation: Brilliant butterfly effect consequence - the one thing Homer can't live without

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, where are my doughnuts?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, doughnuts don't exist in this timeline.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh!

jokes.text: This is indeed a disturbing universe.
jokes.setup: Homer discovering doughnuts don't exist
jokes.punchline: Treating lack of junk food as cosmic horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer perspective on what constitutes a dystopia

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Wait, what do you mean doughnuts don't exist? Not exist? But I... I just ate one this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Lenny
dialogue.line: Homer, that was a bagel.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: A bagel?! No, no, no. It was round, it was sweet, it had a hole in the middle...

dialogue.speaker: Carl
dialogue.line: Dude, that's definitely a doughnut.

dialogue.speaker: Barney
dialogue.line: Actually, I checked with the Department of Agriculture. Doughnuts were declared fictional in 1987.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: NOOOOOO! This is like that time I found out the Tooth Fairy wasn't real, but worse! Much worse! What is life without doughnuts? Why do we even exist? Is anything real anymore? Are YOU real?

dialogue.speaker: Moe
dialogue.line: Homer, you're scaring the other customers.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: We're all just dancing on the edge of an abyss of junk food deprivation! THE VOID STARES BACK, MOE! THE VOID!

jokes.text: Homer, the sky is blue, doughnuts are plentiful, Friday is TGIF night on ABC.
jokes.setup: Homer asking basic reality check questions
jokes.punchline: TV programming as fundamental reality marker
jokes.explanation: Good observation about what constitutes normal reality for this family

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Marge, is TV real?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: What? Of course not, Homer.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Then how do I know what's real?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, you just... you know things.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: But what if everything I know is from TV?

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, TV isn't real life.

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Then why does everything on TV happen exactly like it does in real life?

jokes.text: Close enough.
jokes.setup: Homer settling for this imperfect timeline
jokes.punchline: Accepting mediocrity as good enough
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer philosophy in two words - became widely quoted

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Well, I guess this is as good as it's gonna get. I'm Homer Simpson, and I accept mediocrity!

jokes.text: Oxygen running out.
jokes.setup: Bart in overcrowded detention
jokes.punchline: Prison-like conditions in school punishment
jokes.explanation: Sets up the cannibalism premise but standard overcrowding joke

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Man, this detention is like a prison! We're packed in here like sardines.

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: That's the idea, Simpson. Detention is meant to be unpleasant.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Unpleasant? Principal Skinner, I've seen actual prisons with better conditions!

jokes.text: This sandwich tastes so young and impudent.
jokes.setup: Eating mystery meat in cafeteria
jokes.punchline: Describing human personality traits in food
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor treating student personality as flavor profile

dialogue.speaker: Lunch Lady Doris
dialogue.line: Well, it's got a little bit of everything - some backbone, a heart, a brain...

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: Mommy, what's for dinner?

dialogue.speaker: Lunch Lady Doris
dialogue.line: The same thing that's always for dinner - mystery meat. And today it's got extra personality.

jokes.text: Frau Lunchlady, please to have another Sloppy Jimbo?
jokes.setup: German exchange student ordering lunch
jokes.punchline: Jimbo's name replacing 'Joe' in Sloppy Joe
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay making cannibalism casual through menu naming

dialogue.speaker: Uter
dialogue.line: I vould like a Sloppy Joe, please.

dialogue.speaker: Lunchlady Doris
dialogue.line: We're all out of Sloppy Joes. How about a Sloppy Jimbo?

dialogue.speaker: Uter
dialogue.line: Vhat is the difference?

dialogue.speaker: Jimbo
dialogue.line: Hey!

jokes.text: Oh, relax, kids. I've got a gut feeling Uter's around here somewhere.
jokes.setup: Students suspicious about missing Uter
jokes.punchline: 'Gut feeling' while they're literally eating him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre with 'gut feeling' in cannibalism context

dialogue.speaker: Ralph Wiggum
dialogue.line: Principal Skinner, we're worried about Uter. He's been missing for three days.

dialogue.speaker: Seymour Skinner
dialogue.line: I'm sure it's nothing to worry about. I have a gut feeling he's fine.

dialogue.speaker: Bart Simpson
dialogue.line: A gut feeling? Principal Skinner, we're eating him!

jokes.text: After all, isn't there a little Uter in all of us?
jokes.setup: Continuing to reassure students about Uter
jokes.punchline: Philosophical statement that's literally true since they ate him
jokes.explanation: Perfect double entendre - inspirational platitude that's literally true in cannibalism context

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Now, Uter's not gone. He's just in a better place.

dialogue.speaker: Ralph
dialogue.line: He's in our stomachs.

jokes.text: In fact, you might even say we just ate Uter... and he's in our stomachs right now!
jokes.setup: Building on the 'Uter in all of us' theme
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing the truth while trying to be philosophical
jokes.explanation: Good escalation accidentally revealing the truth through over-explanation

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: You know, there's a little Uter in all of us. We all have that same capacity for... well, for being different, for not fitting in...

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: But the real question is, do we accept that Uter in ourselves, or do we—

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: ...lock him in the meat locker?

jokes.text: Wait, scratch that one.
jokes.setup: Skinner realizing he just confessed to cannibalism
jokes.punchline: Casual attempt to take back major confession
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Skinner's bureaucratic approach to damage control

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Wait, wait. Let me rephrase that.

jokes.text: Listen, kids, you're 8 and 10 years old now. I can't fight all your battles for you.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa report cannibalism at school
jokes.punchline: Marge treats cannibalism as normal childhood problem-solving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge characterization - treating extreme situations as normal parenting moments. Good contrast between serious issue and casual response.

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Mom, there's a cannibal at school!

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: He ate my friend Gerald!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, have you tried talking to him about his feelings? Maybe he's just going through a phase. When you were young, you went through a phase where you only ate paste.

jokes.text: Don't eat me.
jokes.setup: Marge's advice for dealing with cannibalistic school
jokes.punchline: Treating mass murder as simple assertiveness issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental advice reduction of complex problem to simple communication

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Well, if the school's having problems with cannibalism, you just need to be assertive and tell them to stop.

dialogue.speaker: Lisa
dialogue.line: Mom, that's not how you handle mass murder.

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Nonsense, Lisa. A firm 'no thank you' solves everything.

jokes.text: I'm bad at this.
jokes.setup: Willie failing at rescue attempt
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact assessment of incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment from Willie acknowledging his limitations

dialogue.speaker: Willie
dialogue.line: Ach, I've nae got a clue what I'm doin' here.

jokes.text: Yes, I believe I'll start as you've so often suggested... by eating your shorts.
jokes.setup: Skinner about to cannibalize Bart
jokes.punchline: Taking Bart's catchphrase literally
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback making Bart's signature insult literal - perfect dark comedy payoff

dialogue.speaker: Skinner
dialogue.line: Eat my shorts!

dialogue.speaker: Bart
dialogue.line: Ay caramba!

jokes.text: Stupid, cheap weather stripping!
jokes.setup: Inside-out fog seeping into house
jokes.punchline: Homer blaming home improvement issues for supernatural horror
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - treating cosmic horror as mundane household problem

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: D'oh! The fog's coming in through the walls!

dialogue.speaker: Marge
dialogue.line: Homer, what did you do?

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: I may have forgotten to caulk the foundation after that time I tried to build a deck.

jokes.text: The family dog is eyeing Bart's intestine
jokes.setup: Musical continuing with inside-out family
jokes.punchline: Pet treating exposed organs as food
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of body horror comedy with pet behavior

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Mmm... exposed organs.

dialogue.speaker: Santa's Little Helper
dialogue.line: *starts eating Homer's exposed organs*

dialogue.speaker: Homer
dialogue.line: Hey! Bad dog!

tmdb_overview: Lack of beer and cable TV make Homer a crazed killer; the present is altered by a time-traveling Homer; and Principal Skinner turns detention students into food.
insight: Five horror parodies sustain 92.2 score with relentless deadpan subversion.
